basicDetails.schemeName: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram Madhya Pradesh

nodalDepartmentName.label: Public Health and Family Welfare Department

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram" scheme is launched by the Department of Public Health and Medical Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to ensure free and cashless healthcare services to pregnant women and infants up to one year of age in government health institutions by providing free medicines, diagnostics, consumables, blood transfusions, diet, and transport services during pregnancy, delivery, and postnatal care.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Free and cashless delivery - Free treatment for sick newborns and infants up to one year of age. - Free medicines, consumables, and diagnostic services for both mothers and infants. - Free provision of blood and blood transfusion services, if required. - Free diet during hospitalization - Free transport facility
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free healthcare services to pregnant women and infants up to one year of age, including free treatment, medicines, diet, and transport facilities in government hospitals.

children.text: The "Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram" scheme is launched by the Department of Public Health and Medical Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to ensure free and cashless healthcare services to pregnant women and infants up to one year of age in government health institutions by providing free medicines, diagnostics, consumables, blood transfusions, diet, and transport services during pregnancy, delivery, and postnatal care.
Free treatment for sick newborns and infants up to one year of age.
Free medicines, consumables, and diagnostic services for both mothers and infants.
Free provision of blood and blood transfusion services, if required.
The beneficiary should visit the nearest government health institution (District Hospital, Community Health Center, or Primary Health Center) for antenatal or delivery services.

applicationProcess.process_md: - The beneficiary should visit the nearest government health institution (District Hospital, Community Health Center, or Primary Health Center) for antenatal or delivery services.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh. 1. The applicant must be a pregnant woman. 1. The applicant’s child (newborn or infant) should be up to one year of age to receive benefits.

children.text: The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant must be a pregnant woman.
The applicant’s child (newborn or infant) should be up to one year of age to receive benefits.
